frameworks.name: social group structure learning
frameworks.one_line: Observers infer group structure and update stereotypes from agent observations.

frameworks.name: stereotype precision / confidence
frameworks.one_line: New measure indexes group belief precision via perceived agent similarity.

core_question: How do observers discover social group structure and update group stereotypes when encountering deviating agents?
key_assumptions: stereotypes can be characterized by group-level precision and confidence
deviating agents within a group are informative about stereotype precision
perceived similarity among nondeviating agents indexes group-stereotype precision
manipulations: presence of deviating agents within a group
confidence vs similarity measure of group stereotype
experiment variants testing the two predictions
key_findings: deviating agents reduced stereotype confidence in two confidence-measure experiments
perceived similarity among nondeviating agents yielded support in a majority of experiments
the first prediction about confidence was not generally supported
interpretations: perceived inter-agent similarity is a better index of group-stereotype precision than confidence
deviating agents systematically reduce inferred group-stereotype precision
limitations_noted: support for the confidence prediction limited to two experiments
similarity-based precision index emerged from exploratory analysis
